patch.value: Suppress benign extension_service_override_collision when calendar+guide-shell both bundled

In any project with `pm init --with-packages` (or after bundled packages are installed), `pm health` reports a warning: `extension_service_override_collision:output_format:project:builtin-guide-shell:project:builtin-calendar`. Both built-in packages register an `output_format` service; the loader already chains them deterministically (pm-rnpb feature note: `chain output_format service overrides for package renderers`). Demote this expected configuration to debug-level or silence it when the chained loader resolved without ambiguity. Currently fires `ok=true` but produces alarming warning text on every health run.
Default `pm init --with-packages` + `pm health` produces no warnings; collision still flagged for non-builtin conflicts; regression test added

author: codex-20260517
message: Continue benign extension service collision suppression; add focused regression coverage

value.note: suppress collision warnings for chained output_format services
regression for chained output_format and non-chained service collision warnings

value.author: codex-20260517
value.text: Evidence: output_format service overrides are now treated as chained/fall-through registrations, so bundled package renderers no longer produce collision warnings; non-chained service collisions remain warned. Extension-loader regression covers both behaviors, and temp-project init --with-packages + health passed in dogfood.

patch.value: Implemented suppression of benign chained output_format service collision warnings while preserving collision warnings for non-chained services. Verified through extension-loader regression, isolated dogfood, semantic dogfood, and full release gates.
Classified output_format service overrides as chained registrations so expected bundled package renderers do not emit collision warnings.
pm init --with-packages followed by pm health has no benign output_format collision warning while non-chained service collisions still warn.
Extension-loader regression, isolated dogfood, semantic dogfood, and release gates verified the behavior.
